jokes.text: CHARLIE: Attention! Attention, everyone! Gather round, please! Gather round! Mac has a very important announcement!
jokes.setup: Charlie dramatically calling for attention suggesting something genuinely important
jokes.punchline: Sets up Mac's absurd security announcement
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Charlie's eager support of Mac's ridiculous plans

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Attention! Attention, everyone! Gather round, please! Gather round! Mac has a very important announcement!

jokes.text: Now I can determine a subject's threat level without him being able to feel my retinal assessment. Which is a great advantage because the guy can't see how scared Mac is.
jokes.setup: Mac explaining his tactical advantage
jokes.punchline: Dennis immediately identifying Mac's actual motivation is hiding his fear
jokes.explanation: Classic Dennis cutting through Mac's bravado to reveal his insecurity

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Now I can determine a subject's threat level without him being able to feel my retinal assessment. Which is a great advantage because the guy can't see how scared Mac is.

jokes.text: Yes, and... No, that's not what it's about. It's not... Huh? Oh, I thought that you were scared every time you went to go to... That's classified!
jokes.setup: Mac trying to deny Dennis's accurate assessment
jokes.punchline: His flustered response and claiming 'classified' information
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing Mac's inability to handle being exposed

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Yes, and... No, that's not what it's about. It's not... Huh? Oh, I thought that you were scared every time you went to go to... That's classified!

jokes.text: Are they still standing there? No, they walked away. My peripheral is a little compromised.
jokes.setup: Mac wearing sunglasses indoors for 'tactical advantage'
jokes.punchline: Immediately revealing they impair his vision
jokes.explanation: Perfect immediate undermining of Mac's 'tactical advantage' - shows how his plans always backfire

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Are they still standing there? No, they walked away. My peripheral is a little compromised.

jokes.text: Mac revealing tactical sunglasses as his security solution
jokes.setup: Mac promises a security solution to his weight loss problem
jokes.punchline: It's just sunglasses worn indoors
jokes.explanation: Perfect Mac logic - solving an imaginary problem with useless equipment he thinks makes him look tactical

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Mac revealing tactical sunglasses as his security solution

jokes.text: FRANK: Who invited the Jew lawyer? Not Jewish. Hold on, punk. He's clear.
jokes.setup: Frank's casual antisemitism toward the lawyer
jokes.punchline: The awkward correction and Frank's 'security clearance'
jokes.explanation: Typical Frank casual bigotry and the lawyer's uncomfortable correction

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: Who invited the Jew lawyer? Not Jewish. Hold on, punk. He's clear.

jokes.text: Frank immediately doing an 'ocular pat-down' on the lawyer and declaring 'He's clear'
jokes.setup: Mac just explained his ocular pat-down system
jokes.punchline: Frank immediately adopts and misuses it on the lawyer
jokes.explanation: Good callback that shows Frank immediately adopting Mac's ridiculous terminology

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: He's clear

jokes.text: Are you saying that we have to decide whether that old Nazi bitch lives or dies? There's that charm.
jokes.setup: Dee's blunt characterization of their grandfather
jokes.punchline: The lawyer's dry commentary on her 'charm'
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor and the lawyer's deadpan response to Dee's bluntness

dialogue.speaker: Sweet Dee
dialogue.line: Are you saying that we have to decide whether that old Nazi bitch lives or dies?

dialogue.speaker: Attorney
dialogue.line: There's that charm.

jokes.text: ("Ride of the Valkyries" plays)
jokes.setup: Discussion about their Nazi grandfather
jokes.punchline: Wagner's famous Nazi-associated music playing
jokes.explanation: Brilliant musical cue that reinforces the Nazi theme with perfect timing and economy

jokes.text: Oh! Oh! Oh! Oh, Jesus Christ! Ugh! I didn't have a chance to tidy.
jokes.setup: The group entering Pop-Pop's hospital room
jokes.punchline: Frank's casual explanation for the horrific conditions
jokes.explanation: Good visual setup with Frank's typical nonchalance about disgusting conditions

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: Oh! Oh! Oh! Oh, Jesus Christ! Ugh! I didn't have a chance to tidy.

jokes.text: T... Tidy? Frank, the man is rotting.
jokes.setup: Frank's casual 'tidy' comment about the rotting grandfather
jokes.punchline: Dennis's incredulous response to the understatement
jokes.explanation: Good reaction beat highlighting the absurdity of Frank's casual attitude

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: T... Tidy?

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Frank, the man is rotting.

jokes.text: Yeah, what is the overwhelming smell? Oh, that's the soup. Yeah, well, if he doesn't finish, sometimes I leave 'em for him, and then I forget and then months go by.
jokes.setup: The horrible smell in the room
jokes.punchline: Frank's casual explanation about leaving months-old soup
jokes.explanation: Escalating gross-out humor with Frank's typically bizarre logic

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: Yeah, what is the overwhelming smell? Oh, that's the soup. Yeah, well, if he doesn't finish, sometimes I leave 'em for him, and then I forget and then months go by.

jokes.text: Look, the guy always said he had Nazi treasure stashed away. I figure I spend some time with him, he gives it to me.
jokes.setup: Frank's 'caring' for Pop-Pop
jokes.punchline: Revealing his purely mercenary motivation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Frank moment - reveals his 'kindness' was purely selfish

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: Look, the guy always said he had Nazi treasure stashed away. I figure I spend some time with him, he gives it to me.

jokes.text: All right, well, pretty good plan; execution leaves a lot to be desired.
jokes.setup: Frank's plan to get Nazi treasure
jokes.punchline: Dennis's dry assessment with 'execution' pun given the Nazi context
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay with 'execution' in Nazi context, delivered with perfect Dennis smugness

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: All right, well, pretty good plan; execution leaves a lot to be desired.

jokes.text: We're talking about taking a man's life. Yeah, but he was a Nazi, so it's kind of, like, eh...
jokes.setup: Dennis's moral concern about taking a life
jokes.punchline: Dee's casual dismissal because he's a Nazi
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor showing how they casually dismiss moral concerns

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: We're talking about taking a man's life.

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: Yeah, but he was a Nazi, so it's kind of, like, eh...

jokes.text: (gasps): Are you eating the soup?! Is he eating the soup? Yes. I'm sorry. I was hungry.
jokes.setup: The disgusting months-old soup in the grandfather's room
jokes.punchline: Charlie casually eating it despite knowing how old it is
jokes.explanation: Perfect Charlie moment - his complete lack of standards and simple logic

dialogue.speaker: Sweet Dee
dialogue.line: Are you eating the soup?! Is he eating the soup?

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Yes. I'm sorry. I was hungry.

jokes.text: I made a decision to eat the soup. I didn't sit around thinking all day, 'Hey, do I do this? Do I not do this?'
jokes.setup: Charlie defending eating the rotten soup
jokes.punchline: His philosophy of quick decision-making applied to eating garbage
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Charlie logic - applying pseudo-wisdom to eating rotten food

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: I made a decision to eat the soup. I didn't sit around thinking all day, 'Hey, do I do this? Do I not do this?'

jokes.text: I do not like this painting, Charlie. Its smug aura mocks me. It's evil, Charlie. (echoes): Evil.
jokes.setup: Frank's opinion on Charlie's dog painting
jokes.punchline: His dramatic description of the painting as having a 'smug aura' and being evil
jokes.explanation: Iconic Frank moment with perfect absurd language - 'smug aura mocks me' is comedy gold

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: I do not like this painting, Charlie. Its smug aura mocks me. It's evil, Charlie. (echoes): Evil.

jokes.text: You and my dad share this futon?
jokes.setup: Looking at Charlie's living situation
jokes.punchline: Revealing Charlie and Frank share a futon bed
jokes.explanation: Good visual gag about Charlie's pathetic living situation

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: You and my dad share this futon?

jokes.text: By the way, man, this is why we work well together, you know? You see free soup, you make a decision to eat it. It's horrible. It's terrible soup, but we got to stick with our decisions, right? Yeah, I can't go back on it now.
jokes.setup: Mac praising Charlie's decision-making
jokes.punchline: Charlie doubling down on eating terrible soup out of principle
jokes.explanation: Good callback expanding on Charlie's bizarre commitment to bad decisions

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: By the way, man, this is why we work well together, you know? You see free soup, you make a decision to eat it. It's horrible. It's terrible soup, but we got to stick with our decisions, right?

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Yeah, I can't go back on it now.

jokes.text: Okay. They're standing two feet away from us, so they are gonna notice, but let's just run out of here; they'll think we made a quick decision to leave.
jokes.setup: Charlie suggesting they slip away unnoticed
jokes.punchline: His plan to run away in plain sight and claim it was a 'quick decision'
jokes.explanation: Charlie's flawed logic applied to sneaking away - perfect character consistency

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Okay. They're standing two feet away from us, so they are gonna notice, but let's just run out of here; they'll think we made a quick decision to leave.

jokes.text: An original Hitler. Yes! Yeah! Holy shit, dude, this is huge! We are gonna become famous treasure hunters.
jokes.setup: Discovering the painting might be by Hitler
jokes.punchline: Mac's excitement about becoming 'famous treasure hunters'
jokes.explanation: Mac's typical grandiose fantasy about their mundane situation

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: An original Hitler. Yes! Yeah! Holy shit, dude, this is huge! We are gonna become famous treasure hunters.

jokes.text: I'm thinking, Hollywood is gonna make a Da Vinci Code style thriller about us! I can finally be Indiana Jones!
jokes.setup: Mac's excitement about the painting discovery
jokes.punchline: His immediate leap to Hollywood movies and being Indiana Jones
jokes.explanation: Perfect Mac delusion - immediately fantasizing about being an action hero

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: I'm thinking, Hollywood is gonna make a Da Vinci Code style thriller about us! I can finally be Indiana Jones!

jokes.text: What? No-no-no-no-no. It looks so good here. It belongs in a museum, and that's the end of this.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie

dialogue.speaker: Mac

jokes.punchline: Casually mentioning he paralyzed a dog in a 'skirmish'

dialogue.speaker: Cricket
dialogue.line: Yeah, I got into a skirmish with a stray chocolate lab. I won't go into details, but suffice to say, that dog is very paralyzed now.

jokes.text: Yeah, but I'm kind of a dog executioner, so, uh, looks like old Cricket got the last laugh.

dialogue.speaker: Cricket
dialogue.line: Yeah, but I'm kind of a dog executioner, so, uh, looks like old Cricket got the last laugh.

jokes.text: Well, uh, no. You know, yeah, mostly just, you know, cleaning up the poops, you know, washing out their cages, that kind of thing. Ah. Sounds like you're a dog janitor then.

dialogue.speaker: Mac

jokes.text: He's cool. He's not a threat. Phew! I know he's not a threat! I told you, I've assessed him... I've just cleared him.

Frank? What is happening? What are you doing?

jokes.text: These goddamn niggers and Jews are trying to take over this country, and we've got to take it back! Oops. Yeah. That just took a turn.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Oops. Yeah. That just took a turn.

jokes.text: Sieg heil! Sieg heil! Sieg heil! Oh, that's us. Oh, yeah, yeah, I'm doing that.

dialogue.line: Sieg heil! Sieg heil! Sieg heil!

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Oh, that's us.

dialogue.speaker: Sweet Dee
dialogue.line: Oh, yeah, yeah, I'm doing that.

jokes.text: Sieg heil! Sieg heil! Sieg... Okay, that settles it. Let's fry this turkey.

dialogue.speaker: Sweet Dee
dialogue.line: Sieg heil! Sieg heil! Sieg... Okay, that settles it. Let's fry this turkey.

jokes.text: Pick me? What? Put me down! Pick me! I deserve it.

jokes.punchline: Cricket volunteering himself for death

dialogue.speaker: Cricket
dialogue.line: Pick me? What? Put me down! Pick me! I deserve it.

jokes.text: Dennis deciding to let everyone 'die as nature intended. Slowly and painfully'
jokes.setup: They can't bring themselves to make killing decisions
jokes.punchline: Dennis presents prolonged suffering as the natural, moral choice
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark Dennis character work - framing cruelty as moral high ground

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: die as nature intended. Slowly and painfully

jokes.text: Charlie, I'm beginning to think that we may have stumbled onto one of the greatest discoveries of modern times. Follow me here. What if the key to Adolf Hitler's madness was the death of his dog?
jokes.setup: Seeing the dentist's Hitler-inspired office
jokes.punchline: Mac's grand theory about Hitler's motivation being dog death
jokes.explanation: Absurdly elaborate conspiracy theory perfectly fitting Mac's grandiose thinking

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Charlie, I'm beginning to think that we may have stumbled onto one of the greatest discoveries of modern times. Follow me here. What if the key to Adolf Hitler's madness was the death of his dog?

jokes.text: Okay, when he was a kid, he had this little, sweet, little puppy, and he was the happiest chap in all of Germany. Right, 'I love chocolate. I love my dogs.'
jokes.setup: Mac's Hitler theory
jokes.punchline: Charlie's innocent voice for young Hitler
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist humor with Charlie's innocent Hitler impression

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Okay, when he was a kid, he had this little, sweet, little puppy, and he was the happiest chap in all of Germany. Right, 'I love chocolate. I love my dogs.'

jokes.text: I was thinking maybe Mark Wahlberg. What do you think? Hmm, no, you're right, he couldn't match my intensity. Ryan Gosling-- it's locked in.

dialogue.speaker: Mac

jokes.text: This painting clearly causes madness in men. And it must be destroyed.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie

jokes.text: Hitler's painting, the key to the Holocaust, Ryan Gosling playing you? Ridiculous. This has to end now, and so I have the final solution.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Hitler's painting, the key to the Holocaust, Ryan Gosling playing you? Ridiculous. This has to end now, and so I have the final solution.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie

jokes.text: You are killing a man. You know that, right? There's a lot of mercy in this decision. I commend it. Oh, you commend-- well, you know, I mean, technically we gave the order to him to, to give to you, so...

dialogue.line: Oh, you commend-- well, you know, I mean, technically we gave the order to him to, to give to you, so...

jokes.text: Uh, how long until he's not breathing on his own? Until he perishes? Right. Could be months, weeks, days. Who knows? He's a fighter, this one. Son of a bitch.
